title: Bind premerge release freeze to the candidate tree
outOfScope: Changing concrete source identity or HEAD rejection semantics
Creating a separate budget-only Work Item

sources.reason: Premerge freeze currently hashes the pre-merge default branch instead of the candidate tree.
Post-merge gate compares candidate identity and canonical tree.
Regression coverage for finalizer lifecycle and identity behavior.
Documented candidate versus published release lifecycle.

riskAssessment.reason: A premerge candidate must remain valid after a clean PR merge while final publication remains source-bound.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized stopping publication to correct the root release-process defect before resuming.

preReviewWarnings: Do not weaken fail-closed identity checks or publish from this Work Item.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record pre-edit and pre-finish evidence for the release lifecycle correction.

acceptance: Premerge finalization computes canonical source tree and archive digest from the clean candidate Work Item branch tree while retaining controlled origin identity for post-merge resolution.
A clean PR merge with the same tree passes release preflight; stale or conflicting trees remain fail-closed.
Focused tests, full Finish/archive/PR/merge/ai-close lifecycle complete; publication remains out of scope.
guidelines: Fail closed on identity or tree mismatch.
Keep candidate and published evidence distinct.
Do not rewrite immutable archive evidence.

intent.problem: The release-preparation PR generated freeze evidence from the old origin/main before merge, so the merged candidate failed its own post-close source-tree audit.
constraints: Use the current clean candidate branch tree for premerge digest generation.
Keep origin/main as the controlled identity resolved after merge.
Do not accept HEAD as a release identity.
intent.rationale: Separate candidate-tree materialization from post-merge identity resolution so PR-first release preparation has a closed lifecycle without weakening source binding.

rawUserRequest: When a release process problem is found, stop publication and fix the root process before continuing.

declaredIntent.summary: Make premerge release freeze evidence survive a clean PR merge by binding hashes to the candidate tree and identity to the post-merge controlled default branch.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Premerge hashes are generated from the clean candidate tree, not the stale default branch.
Controlled origin/main identity resolves to the merged default branch and passes when the tree is unchanged.
A changed candidate/default tree remains blocked before publication.

repaymentRecords: release_premerge_candidate_tree_fix|pythonLines|40450|40550|user|2026-07-24|Reserve the bounded finalizer and regression correction required to close the observed premerge/postmerge tree-binding gap; repay during final plan cleanup.
release_premerge_candidate_tree_fix|archiveGrowth|523|524|user|2026-07-24|Reserve this functional root-process correction archive; repay during final plan cleanup.
budgetImpact.reason: Functional release-process correction; no separate budget-only Work Item.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized root-cause process correction before continuing publication.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore the prior finalizer behavior and regression coverage.
